stem: A network performance study measures signal latency for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is below 50ms for 99% of packets. The sample standard deviation is 3.62 ms (sample variance = 13.14). Estimate the true population standard deviation of signal latency and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 3.62 ms. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [2.76, 5.29]. Point estimate: 3.62 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (3.62) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
